title: MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES - 211info
text: CRIME VICTIM SERVICE CENTER MULTICULTURAL COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use disorder (SUD) treatment: screening, assessment, and individual and group counseling. In-person and telehealth options are available. 4531 SE Belmont Street Suite 300 Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Low-income households with a focus on those who have CareOregon (OHP) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 731-9589 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 731-9589 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Varies by service, accepts Medicaid (OHP) and sliding scale is available. Can not bill private insurances. DOCUMENTS: Proof of family size and income if applying for sliding scale AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 15 Services: Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Medicaid Recipients Other Services Offered At This Location: LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - CRIME VICTIM ADVOCACY PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CRIME VICTIM SERVICE CENTER
